title: scirpus cernuus subsp. californicus
intro: Scirpus cernuus subsp. californicus, also known as California bulrush or California wool-grass, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Scirpus cernuus subsp. californicus include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that are used to treat various health conditions.
